threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'thank you' to the bus driver? Just a nod around here, mate. You’ll catch on soon enough.
Caught a night bus from Brixton and a guy argued with the driver about the 'real' Brixton Pie. He swore it’s a secret recipe, no one else knows.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The first time I heard a spontaneous rap battle in the park, with kids gathering around like it was a festival. That’s when it hit me.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You know you’re in Kreuzberg when the kebab shop has a rotating art installation on the ceiling and serves homemade sauces you can't find anywhere else.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they cringe at the thought of a hipster taco truck lining up in front of La Taqueria.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 38 L last week packed tighter than a sardine tin, but the guy next to me was serenading the crowd with an oldie. That’s Muni life, eh?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think every bar here serves the same tapas, but the locals are fiercely loyal to their neighborhood favorites, each with secret recipes.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their confused expressions at the wild flow of traffic on Carrer verda. The locals glide through like it's a dance.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it ‘Broadway Market’ instead of just ‘The Market.’ Everyone knows it’s not a Broadway when you have to share the path with hipsters on bikes.
Caught the 196 last night. A guy in a bright orange puffer jacket was passionately arguing with a pigeon. The bird didn’t budge.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a hipster with a falafel in one hand and a vintage record in the other, I knew I was home. It's like the universe curated my neighborhood.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Neukölln can you find a kebab shop that doubles as a gallery on Thursdays. Art and shawarma, all under one roof. Pure Berlin spirit.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Real locals know that the best tacos are from the truck on 19th, not the sit-down places. We don’t waste time on overpriced burritos, either.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a taco costume handing out burritos on the 14 Mission. Only in San Francisco does Muni become a moving fiesta at rush hour.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all Gràcia streets are lively, but some hidden alleys are eerily silent at night, revealing a softer, forgotten side.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still taking selfies with every street corner, completely oblivious to the locals rushing past.
